name: arpwatch
title: arpwatch
description:
Ethernet/FDDI station activity monitor
Arpwatch maintains a database of Ethernet MAC addresses seen on the
network, with their associated IP pairs.  Alerts the system administrator
via e-mail if any change happens, such as new station/activity,
flip-flops, changed and re-used old addresses.
.
If you want to maintain a list authorized MAC addresses manually, take a look
at the arpalert package which may fit your needs better.

commands.name: arp2ethers
commands.description: convert arpwatch address database to ethers file format

commands.name: arpfetch
commands.description: obtain ethernet/ip address pairings via snmp

commands.name: arpsnmp
commands.description: keep track of ethernet/ip address pairings

commands.name: arpwatch
commands.description: keep track of ethernet/ip address pairings

commands.name: massagevendor
commands.description: convert the ethernet vendor codes master list to arpwatch format

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use arp2ethers because Ethernet/FDDI station activity monitor.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use arp2ethers as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `arp2ethers [ arp.dat file ]` as documented upstream for arp2ethers.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair arp2ethers with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `arp2ethers --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: arp2ethers - convert arpwatch address database to ethers file format
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install arpwatch. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `arp2ethers [ arp.dat file ]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Ethernet/FDDI station activity monitor.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: arp2ethers - convert arpwatch address database to ethers file format.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install arpwatch
